note_data.title: Grammar — Tenses
keyPoints: Tense is the form of a verb that shows the time of an action or state of being
There are three main tenses: Present, Past, and Future, each with four forms
Present Tense expresses actions happening now or habitual actions
Past Tense shows actions that have already happened or completed states
Future Tense indicates actions that will happen or states that will exist
Each tense has four aspects: Simple, Continuous, Perfect, and Perfect Continuous
Simple tenses express basic actions without emphasis on duration or completion
Continuous tenses show ongoing actions using 'be' + present participle (-ing form)
Perfect tenses indicate completed actions using 'have/has/had' + past participle
Sequence of tenses must be maintained in complex sentences for grammatical accuracy

definitions.definition: Tense is the form of a verb that shows the time of an action or state of being
Present Tense is used to express actions that are happening now, habitual actions, or general truths
Past Tense is used to express actions that have already happened or states that existed in the past
Future Tense is used to express actions that will happen or states that will exist in the future
Perfect Tense shows completed actions in relation to a specific time, formed with auxiliary verbs 'have', 'has', or 'had' plus past participle

importantQuestions.question: Define tense and name its three main types.
importantQuestions.answer: Tense is the form of a verb that shows the time of an action or state of being. The three main types are: Present Tense, Past Tense, and Future Tense.

importantQuestions.question: Write a detailed note on Present Tense with examples.
importantQuestions.answer: Present Tense expresses actions happening now or habitual actions. It has four forms: (1) Simple Present: I study in Karachi Grammar School. (2) Present Continuous: Students are playing cricket in the ground. (3) Present Perfect: The government has built Tarbela Dam. (4) Present Perfect Continuous: It has been raining in Sindh for three hours. Simple Present shows routine actions, Present Continuous shows ongoing actions, Present Perfect shows completed actions with present relevance, and Present Perfect Continuous shows actions continuing from past to present.

mcqs.question: Which tense is used in the sentence 'The train will have left Karachi station by 6 PM'?
Identify the tense: 'Ahmed has been studying at Sindh University for two years.'
The sentence 'I go to school daily' is an example of:

commonMistakes: Students often confuse Simple Past with Present Perfect when using time expressions
Incorrect use of auxiliary verbs in forming Perfect tenses (mixing 'have' and 'had')
Using Present Continuous for habitual actions instead of Simple Present
Forgetting to change tense in reported speech according to sequence of tenses rules
Mixing up Past Continuous with Past Perfect Continuous formations
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1 short question in every BSEK paper
Memorize the exact definitions as they appear in STBB textbook
Practice identifying tenses in sentences with Pakistani context examples
Sequence of tenses questions appear frequently in long questions section
Always provide examples when writing notes on tenses for full marks
formulas: Present Continuous: Subject + am/is/are + V1+ing + Object
Present Perfect: Subject + have/has + V3 + Object
Future Simple: Subject + will + V1 + Object
note_data.keyDiagram: Tense Chart showing three main tenses (Present, Past, Future) with their four forms each: Simple, Continuous, Perfect, and Perfect Continuous. Each form should show structure formula and one example sentence using Pakistani context.
